title: aesculus octandra f. virginica
intro: Aesculus octandra f. virginica, also known as the yellow buckeye, is a plant species within the Aesculus genus, specifically a variety of the buckeye tree. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The yellow buckeye is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving pain, as well as aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
